title_en: Ahmed al-Sharaa is quashing Syria’s vast captagon trade
title_zh: 艾哈迈德·沙阿拉正在取缔叙利亚规模庞大的卡普他贡交易
rubric_en: But pills that cost cents to produce still sell for $25
rubric_zh: 但生产只要几美分的药片在沙特却可能卖到25美元

paragraphs.en: Among bashar al-assad’s many crimes as Syria’s dictator was turning his country into a narco-state. Captagon, an amphetamine, became Syria’s largest export. Mr Assad’s neighbours resented his role as drug-pusher-in-chief. To their relief, Ahmed al-Sharaa, Syria’s new president, seems eager to stem the flow of captagon in the region. But as a result, the trade is becoming more decentralised.
paragraphs.zh: 在巴沙尔·阿萨德作为叙利亚独裁者期间的诸多罪行中，他正将国家变成一个“毒品国”。卡普他贡（冰毒类安非他命）已成为叙利亚最大的出口品。阿萨德的邻国厌恶他扮演“头号毒枭”的角色。令他们松了口气的是，叙利亚新总统艾哈迈德·沙阿拉似乎急于遏制该地区的卡普他贡流通。然而，结果是这条黑市贸易正在变得更加去中心化。

paragraphs.en: Captagon, also known as fenethylline, was developed in the 1960s to treat hyperactivity in children and narcolepsy. In 1986 it was banned internationally because it was addictive. Today poor labourers use it to fuel long shifts. Some people take it to suppress hunger. Traumatised soldiers self-medicate with it. The Gulf elite use it as a party drug.
paragraphs.zh: 卡普他贡，又称费尼硫胺（fenethylline），最初在20世纪60年代被开发出来，用于治疗儿童多动症和发作性睡病。1986年，它因具有成瘾性而被国际禁用。如今，贫困劳动者拿它来支撑超长工作班次；一些人则用它来抑制饥饿；受创伤影响的士兵会用它自我用药；海湾地区的上层人士则把它当作派对药物。

paragraphs.en: It is a lucrative trade. A pill costs cents to produce but may sell for $25 in Saudi Arabia. The global trade may be worth as much as $10bn. The un says between 2019 and 2025 as much as 80% of global seizures originated in Syria.
paragraphs.zh: 这是一门利润巨大的生意。一颗药片的生产成本只有几美分，却可能在沙特阿拉伯以25美元的价格出售。全球这类交易的规模可能高达100亿美元。联合国数据显示，在2019年至2025年间，全球没收的卡普他贡中，最多有80%来源于叙利亚。

paragraphs.en: As part of his efforts to win international legitimacy and convince outsiders to unlock reconstruction funds, Mr Sharaa has cracked down on the trade. In the first year of his rule his forces seized over 500m pills and dismantled 16 industrial laboratories. In June last year Anas Khattab, the interior minister, declared that captagon was no longer being produced in Syria.
paragraphs.zh: 为了赢得国际认可并说服外界释放重建资金，沙阿拉已对这一行业展开打击。就任第一年，他的部队查获了超过5亿粒药片，并拆除了16家工业化实验室。去年6月，内政部长阿纳斯·哈塔布宣布，叙利亚已不再生产卡普他贡。

paragraphs.en: He spoke too soon. Over the past year Syrian authorities have seized over 80m pills. Some are Assad-era stockpiles. But new production plants are being built, too. Most are in Sweida, a southern province dominated by Druze militias that have clashed with government forces. Remnants of the Fourth Armoured Division, an elite army unit led by Mr Assad’s brother that oversaw the state’s labs, have fled there and started making the pills again. “Sweida 24”, a local news outlet, reports that 12-15 labs operate in the province.
paragraphs.zh: 他言犹未尽。过去一年里，叙利亚当局已查获超过8000万片这种药。里面有些是阿萨德时期储存下来的库存。但新的生产工厂也在陆续建起。大多数位于斯韦达，这个由德鲁兹武装主导的南部省份，该武装与政府军有过冲突。阿萨德兄弟领导、第4装甲师残部——这个曾管理国家实验室的精锐部队——也逃到那里，重新开始生产这些药片。当地媒体“斯韦达24”报道，该省有12到15家实验室在运作。

paragraphs.en: Much of what is produced in Sweida is smuggled into Jordan, the only overland route to markets in the Gulf. Syria and Jordan share over 360km of desert borderlands and a web of tribal trade routes. Interceptions along the border have risen almost four-fold since the Druze-led National Guard took control of the province in July last year. In January Jordan and Syria set up a joint security committee to tackle the trade in drugs and arms. They also deployed more forces to the border. In May Jordan conducted its fifth round of air strikes on production facilities in Sweida. Officials say they were in co-ordination with Syrian authorities. Smugglers are adapting by using gps-guided balloons loaded with pills, which Jordanian forces shoot down, with Mr Sharaa’s blessing.
paragraphs.zh: 在斯韦达生产的许多药片都会被走私到约旦，这是通往海湾市场的唯一陆路通道。叙利亚与约旦之间共有360公里以上的沙漠边境及一张错综复杂的部落贸易路线网。自去年7月德鲁兹人主导的国家卫队接管该省后，边境沿线的查获案件几乎增长了四倍。1月，约旦和叙利亚成立了联合安全委员会来打击毒品和武器交易，并且在边境增派了更多部队。5月，约旦对斯韦达的生产设施实施了第五轮空袭，官方称这是与叙利亚当局协同进行的。走私者正在改用装有药片的GPS导向气球来规避打击，这些气球会被约旦军方击落，而这得到了沙拉阿先生的默许。

paragraphs.en: Production is spreading. Hizbullah, the Iran-backed militia in Lebanon, started making captagon in 2006 to replenish its coffers after a war with Israel, which gouged its finances. It has turned to the drugs business once again. In the past six months a quarter of all captagon seized by Syrian authorities has come from Lebanon, says Charles Lister, an analyst. In the past year Lebanese authorities have raided three laboratories in Baalbek, near the Syrian border. They also caught smugglers trying to get production equipment into the Bekaa Valley, a Hizbullah redoubt. A year ago a joint Iraqi-Lebanese operation shut a captagon laboratory in the valley, said to be one of the region’s largest.
paragraphs.zh: 生产正在扩散。黎巴嫩的真主党在2006年与以色列的战争中财政受损后，曾重启卡普他根生产以填补国库。如今它又回到了毒品生意。分析师查尔斯·利斯特称，在过去六个月里，叙利亚当局查获的所有卡普他根中有四分之一来自黎巴嫩。过去一年，黎巴嫩当局已在靠近叙利亚边境的巴勒贝克地区查封了三家实验室。他们还逮捕了试图将生产设备运往白朗谷的走私者，那是黎巴嫩真主党的重要据点。去年，一次伊拉克与黎巴嫩联合行动关闭了位于白朗谷的一家卡普他根工厂，据称是该地区最大的工厂之一。

paragraphs.en: Labs have been found in Kuwait and Egypt. In February last year a factory making about 100,000 pills per hour was seized in Sudan in territory previously held by the Rapid Support Forces, a paramilitary group. Last June Yemeni authorities seized 1.5m captagon pills being moved from Sanaa, controlled by the Houthi rebels, to Saudi Arabia. This May Indian authorities raided a production network spanning the country, a first for South Asia.
paragraphs.zh: 科威特和埃及也发现了生产实验室。去年2月，在苏丹一处此前由“支援力量”（Rapid Support Forces）控制的地区，警方查获了一家每小时可生产约10万片的工厂。去年6月，也门当局查获了150万片卡普他根，这批药片正从受到胡塞叛军控制的萨那被运往沙特阿拉伯。5月，印度当局突袭并取缔了跨国生产网络，这是南亚地区首次发生此类案件。

paragraphs.en: Captagon can lead to heart and brain damage. But the Middle East remains a ravenous consumer. The disruptions in supply are driving up street prices, so users are turning to crystal methamphetamine, an even more destructive stimulant. The region’s appetite for cheap synthetic highs shows no sign of abating. ■
paragraphs.zh: 卡普他贡可能导致心脏和大脑损伤。但中东依然是这类毒品的“重度”消费区。供应中断正在推高街头售价，因此吸食者正转向冰毒——这种兴奋剂更具破坏性。这个地区对低价合成兴奋剂的需求没有减弱迹象。■

paragraphs.en: Sign up to the Middle East Dispatch, a weekly newsletter that keeps you in the loop on a fascinating, complex and consequential part of the world.
paragraphs.zh: 订阅《中东日报（Middle East Dispatch）》，这份每周通讯将让你持续了解世界上一块迷人、复杂且影响重大的区域。
